---
pagetitle: "Nicole M. Lee"
---

<!-- Hero is raw HTML on purpose: a Markdown `#` heading gets promoted by Quarto into a
     full-width title block, which tears the name out of this flex row. Raw HTML keeps the
     name, tagline, and headshot together in one row. 📷 headshot → nicole.png -->
```{=html}
<!--
  The design and template of this site (its visual and interaction design: theme, colours,
  typography, and the card / hover / layout system) is the original work of Nicole M. Lee and
  may be reused with credit under CC BY 4.0.
  All written text and images are her content, all rights reserved, and are NOT for reuse.
  Source and full terms: https://github.com/mnicolee/Portfolio  (see LICENSE and AGENTS.md)
-->
<div class="hero">
  <div class="hero-text">
    <div class="hero-name" role="heading" aria-level="1">Nicole M. Lee, Ph.D.</div>
    <p class="tagline">Behavioral neuroscience · Optogenetics · Hardware prototyping<br>Estimation statistics · Data analysis &amp; visualization</p>
  </div>
  <img src="nicole.png" class="headshot" width="300" alt="Nicole M. Lee">
</div>
```

I'm a **neuroscientist** in the [**ACC Lab**](https://www.claridgechang.net/about.html) (Adam Claridge-Chang) at Duke-NUS
Medical School, where I completed my PhD and now continue as a Research Fellow, building
**optogenetic methods to study how small circuits in the *Drosophila* brain shape behavior**.

Before the flies, I completed my B.S. in Biochemistry at the University of Washington, Seattle, and
worked on several projects: the
[biochemistry of collagen degradation](https://orthop.washington.edu/research/ourlabs/collagen-biology-and-genetic-disorders-lab.html),
[mTOR and kappa-opioid receptor signaling](https://depts.washington.edu/uwconte/chavkinlab/),
[asymmetric cell division](https://cabernardlab.org/overview), and [cancer drug-resistance](https://csi.nus.edu.sg/).
Beyond the science, I paint 🎨, game 🎮, and build apps 📱.

[]{.section-rule .dots}

```{=html}
<div class="whatido-fields">
  <a class="wi-field" href="research.qmd" aria-label="The neuroscience">
    <span class="wi-med"><svg class="wi-ico wi-ico--brain" viewBox="0 0 48 48" aria-hidden="true">
      <path d="M24 10 C20 7 14 8 13 13 C8 13 6 19 10 22 C7 25 9 31 14 30 C15 34 22 34 24 30 C26 34 33 34 34 30 C39 31 41 25 38 22 C42 19 40 13 35 13 C34 8 28 7 24 10 Z"/>
      <path d="M24 10 L24 30"/><path d="M17 16 C20 18 20 22 17 24"/><path d="M31 16 C28 18 28 22 31 24"/>
    </svg></span>
    <h3>The neuroscience</h3>
    <p>How circuits in the <em>Drosophila</em> brain shape what an animal does, and the light-driven tools I build and validate to switch those neurons on and off (kalium channelrhodopsins, opto-GPCRs).</p>
  </a>
  <a class="wi-field" href="hardware.qmd" aria-label="The nuts and bolts">
    <span class="wi-med"><svg class="wi-ico wi-ico--wrench" viewBox="0 0 48 48" aria-hidden="true">
      <path d="M45.4 38 l-18.2 -18.2 c1.8 -4.6 .8 -10 -3 -13.8 -4 -4 -10 -4.8 -14.8 -2.6 L18 12 12 18 3.2 9.4 C.8 14.2 1.8 20.2 5.8 24.2 c3.8 3.8 9.2 4.8 13.8 3 l18.2 18.2 c.8 .8 2 .8 2.8 0 l4.6 -4.6 c1 -.8 1 -2.2 .2 -2.8 z"/>
    </svg></span>
    <h3>The nuts &amp; bolts</h3>
    <p>Designing the rigs that deliver the light and capture behavior: arenas, illumination, and high-throughput tracking pipelines.</p>
  </a>
  <a class="wi-field" href="papers/dabest/index.qmd" aria-label="The numbers">
    <span class="wi-med"><svg class="wi-ico wi-ico--plot" viewBox="0 0 48 48" aria-hidden="true">
      <path d="M6 35 H42"/><path d="M9 35 C18 35 18 11 24 11 C30 11 30 35 39 35"/>
      <path d="M24 15 V37"/><circle class="dot" cx="24" cy="25" r="3"/>
    </svg></span>
    <h3>The numbers</h3>
    <p>Estimation statistics over p-values: reporting effect sizes with bootstrap confidence intervals rather than significance stars, plus the analysis pipelines that turn fly-tracking data into behavioral metrics.</p>
  </a>
</div>
```
